title: sideroxylon capiri subsp. tempisque
intro: Sideroxylon capiri subsp. tempisque, also known as the Tempisque ebony, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Costa Rica and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential use as an anti-cancer agent and an antiviral agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared through herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
